summary: Continued Corneria stage decompilation from PR #2381, with a large pass over src/melee/gr/grcorneria.c and supporting Ground variable headers. The PR added/rewrote substantial Arwing and Corneria behavior logic, fully matching 8 grcorneria functions and improving 10 more, including large partial matches for grCorneria_801DED50 and grCorneria_801DCE1C. It also corrected several struct field types and added a second Corneria ground-vars overlay to model callbacks that reuse the same Ground gv union bytes with a different interpretation.
what_changed: Added includes for it/items/itarwinglaser.h and pl/player.h in grcorneria.c, consistent with new Arwing laser/item spawning and player-coordinate logic in the diff.
Changed grCn_StageData fields at offsets 0x1C, 0x20, 0x28, 0x2C, and 0x68 from s32 to f32, reflecting float use in stage timing/position/chance calculations.
Refined grCn_Data layout: x3D8 changed from s32[9][3] to flat s32[27], and the former pad at 0x478 was split into x478[3] plus padding.

Added a nontrivial implementation of grCorneria_801DCE1C for Arwing spawn scheduling, slot counting, unique type selection, far/near checks, and handling the Stage_80225194() == 0x46 case by clearing Arwing gobj slots.
Reworked Arwing helper logic such as grCn_CheckFar and grCn_PickUniqueType with signed rand_id comparisons and condition-order changes that helped matching.

Implemented grCorneria_801DED50, a large Arwing/Corneria callback handling linked Arwing state, animation state/timers, position synchronization, blast-zone checks, laser firing, player targeting, audio calls, material effects, and cleanup of child gobjs.
Converted several grCorneria callbacks to use grCn_Data* data = &grCn_803E1D38 locals, reducing repeated global accesses and affecting codegen.

Converted grCorneria_801DE8E4 and grCorneria_801DF8D0 from generic grCorneria_GroundVars pointer-punning to the new grCorneria_GroundVars2 overlay, using explicit HSD_GObj* fields and switch cases for Arwing type ranges 1-9 and 10-13.
Changed grArwing_GroundVars fields xE0/xE4/xE8 from separate floats to Vec3 xE0, and updated code to write gp->gv.arwing.xE0.x/y/z and pass &gp->gv.arwing.xE0 to HSD_JObjSetTranslate.
Added struct grCorneria_GroundVars2 to types.h and added it to the Ground gv union as corneria2.
Adjusted grCorneria_GroundVars signedness and types: x10C/x110 became s32 and x118 became s8.
Introduced Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX inline for grCorneria_801E1060, manually writing jobj->translate.x and dirtying the matrix to match expected codegen.

Updated existing callbacks including grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801DE568, grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801E1348, grCorneria_801E1970, grCorneria_801E2228, and grCorneria_801E2598 for better matching.
decomp.dev reported 8 new full matches and +4492 matched bytes overall for GALE01, with matched code increasing by +0.12% to 62.18%.
smart_moves: Adding grCorneria_GroundVars2 was a strong struct-modeling move: the diff explicitly documents that callbacks 2 and 10 overlap grCorneria_GroundVars in the gv union but interpret fields as pointers/integers instead of floats.
Representing grArwing_GroundVars xE0/xE4/xE8 as a Vec3 eliminated manual float-field aliasing and aligned with HSD_JObjSetTranslate usage.
Flattening x3D8 to s32[27] matched observed indexing like data->x3D8[new_state * 2 + grp_off + 15], avoiding an artificial 2D layout that did not fit all access patterns.
Using local grCn_Data* data pointers made repeated global accesses cleaner and appears to have helped code generation in multiple functions.
Replacing goto/default-style range logic with switch cases for Arwing types 1-9 and 10-13 made the behavior clearer while preserving matching-oriented control flow.
The Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX inline is a targeted matching tactic: it mimics only the needed field write and matrix-dirty behavior instead of calling the library helper directly.
Condition-order and expression-shape edits such as if (-10.0f < pos.x && pos.x < 2000.0f), ABS usage, M_TAU usage, and explicit casts show careful attention to compiler output.
decomp_lessons: When one Ground gv union region is reused by different callbacks with different field meanings, prefer adding a separate typed overlay rather than continuing to pointer-pun through unrelated fields.
Do not assume adjacent float fields are independent scalars if call sites pass their address as a vector; model them as Vec3 when translation APIs consume them together.
Stage data fields initially typed as s32 may need to become f32 when they feed float comparisons, timers, probabilities, offsets, or scale calculations.
Large stage actors such as Corneria Arwings often need both the parent stage object state and spawned child gobj state modeled in headers before functions become tractable.
For matching, sometimes an inlined fake version of a simple HSD helper can reproduce field writes and dirty-flag side effects better than calling the real helper.
Flattened arrays can match better than multidimensional arrays when the original code indexes a table with computed linear offsets.
Keep signedness honest: changes from u32/u8 to s32/s8 in types.h indicate that small field signedness can affect casts, comparisons, and calls.
Use bot match reports as evidence for prioritization: this PR turned several near matches into 100% and added two major partial decompilations, even though one .sdata item regressed slightly.
naming_conventions: Stage-local globals and structs retain the grCn_ prefix, such as grCn_Data, grCn_StageData, grCn_803E1D38, and grCn_804D69A0.
Unidentified functions keep address-based names such as grCorneria_801DCE1C and grCorneria_801DED50.
Helper inlines use descriptive grCn_ names when behavior is understood, e.g. grCn_RandRange, grCn_CheckFar, grCn_SpawnArwing, and grCn_PickUniqueType.
The new overlay was named grCorneria_GroundVars2 rather than overloading grCorneria_GroundVars, explicitly signaling alternate interpretation of the same union bytes.
Unidentified struct members still use offset-style names like xC8, xF4, x100, x478, and x3D8, with comments documenting byte offsets in types.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK adjustments, including PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(24), to preserve expected stack layout.
Used local aliases such as grCn_Data* data = &grCn_803E1D38 to alter load patterns and reduce repeated global references.
Introduced Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX to inline a jobj null check, assert path, direct translate.x write, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty call under JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT conditions.
Changed control-flow expression shapes, including switch statements for stage IDs and Arwing type ranges, explicit default cases, and reordered comparisons.
Used ABS and M_TAU instead of hand-written negation and 2.0 * M_PI loops in grCorneria_801DE568/related angle normalization paths.
Used explicit casts such as (s32) grCn_804D69A0->x68 and (s8) gp->gv.corneria.x119 where field types or call signatures required matching.
Replaced string literal file name in OSReport with __FILE__, preserving the same reported file while improving source form/codegen.
Modeled direct user_data access in some places, e.g. Ground* gp = gobj->user_data, instead of always using GET_GROUND.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body only states: "Draft PR, continuing from #2381".
decomp.dev bot reported +4492 matched bytes, 8 new full matches, 10 unmatched-item improvements, and one .sdata regression of -4 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Look up PR #2381 for the previous Corneria context this PR explicitly continues from.
Search later PRs for grCorneria_GroundVars2 to see whether its fields were renamed beyond xC8/xF4/x100.
Search later PRs for grCorneria_801DED50 and grCorneria_801DCE1C to see whether the remaining unmatched portions reached 100%.
Search for Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX reuse or removal after more HSD_JObj helpers were matched.
Audit subsequent changes to grCn_Data.x3D8 and x478 for better table names.
